top_signals.title: Why a Supply Crunch From Iran Could Send Oil Back Below $40 a Barrel
top_signals.summary: There have recently been many warnings about near-term oil shortages stemming from the conflict in Iran. Most analysts assume that shortages mean higher prices. As I will explain, the dynamics of a self-organizing economy suggest the opposite outcome — lower prices, deepening recession, and shortages of goods and services that have little to do with price. A cartoon scene featuring a bluebird on a cliff, looking confidently at a surprised coyote who is about to fall off the edge, with a yellow sky in the background. Figure 1. Wile E. Coyote…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: US Crude Oil Inventories Keep Falling As Hormuz Flows Slow To Start
top_signals.summary: The American Petroleum Institute (API) estimated that crude oil inventories in the United States fell by 6.072 million barrels in the week ending June 26. In the week prior, US crude oil inventories fell by 765,000 barrels. Although commercial crude oil inventories excluding the SPR have been falling rapidly for more than two months, shedding 59.4 million barrels over the last eleven weeks, US crude inventories are only down 8 million barrels so far this year, according to API data, kept in check by draws from the SPR. For the week ending June…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

alerts.title: Phantom doubles down on perpetual futures with hire of Hyperliquid market builders
Japan triples departure tax in push to combat overtourism
alerts.summary: Previously set at ¥1,000, the tax has risen to ¥3,000 for all travelers departing Japan, regardless of nationality.

alerts.title: India’s fiscal deficit reaches 9.6% of annual target in two months
alerts.summary: India’s fiscal deficit stood at 1.62 trillion rupees for the April-May period, representing 9.6% of the full-year estimate for the financial year ending March 31, according to government data released Tuesday. The government has established a fiscal deficit target of 4.3% of GDP for the 2026/27 financial year, equivalent to 16.96 trillion rupees. Source: Investing.com
